content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (like ICM from Topic A) can be repurposed to prioritize which new samples an active learning agent stores in its long-term memory, rather than just using them to encourage exploration. This creates a self-sustaining "curiosity compression loop" where an agent only retains memory traces of the unexpected, information-dense observations it actively seeks out, rather than overloading its context window with redundant, predictable data.
